view_meta: Node inspector (golden signals · dependencies · blast radius) + incident master-clock
purpose: Estate depth: a node-inspector detail panel (identity, health, golden-signal sparklines, active alerts, infragraph dependencies with confidence weights, predicted blast radius) opened from the topology graph, plus the incident master-clock — one shared-time SVG with an event lane (alert → hypotheses → prediction.commit → approve → execute → verify) pixel-aligned above the session key-metric lane, showing the observed territory series (steel, solid) against the AI's predicted post-execution envelope (indigo, dashed) with T0 marked across both lanes.
notes: Smoke-tested under Node with DOM stubs and the documented fixtures: all three entry points build, dependency/blast math verified (edge [a,b,w] read as "a depends on b, confidence w"; blast radius = transitive downstream closure with multiplied confidences, cycle-safe via best-confidence map, hop<=5, floor 0.05). ASSUMPTIONS where the foundation docs arrived empty: (1) sparkline is called as sparkline(values,{w,h,color,stroke}) through the est2Spark adapter — if the primitive exists and returns an element it is used verbatim; otherwise a token-faithful local fallback renders (steel solid line + 9% area + last-point dot), so nothing breaks either way. (2) The master-clock metric lane is rendered directly with svgEl on the SAME x-scale function as the event lane instead of through metricPanel/areaChart, because pixel-exact shared-time alignment across both lanes (plus the T0/alert verticals spanning them) is the whole point of the "one incident clock"; if areaChart later accepts {xDomain,width,markers} it can replace that block. Design language: observed series/sparklines = --territory solid; predicted envelope, infragraph confidence bars, hop chips = --map dashed/indigo; execute marker = --hazard; verify tick + out-of-envelope overlay = verdict tokens; every status is glyph+label+color (● ▲ ■ / ✓ ◐ ✕); all numerics tabular mono; fixtures labeled "fixture"; actuation buttons present but disabled with a MUTATION OFF note; live-dot pulse and nothing else animates, gated on reduceMotion. incidentTimeline synthesizes a deterministic per-session clock (seeded by session id) from session.band/verdict/alerts/reason since stage timestamps are not in the fixture contract — real timestamps can be dropped into est2IncidentModel later without touching the renderer. Contract names estateNodeDetail/incidentTimeline are global per your spec; every other symbol is est2*/EST2_*/.e2- namespaced.
